findings.title: Do not commit AI conversation transcripts
findings.impact: The new repository artifact records assistant internal reasoning and tool-use payloads. Even when this instance does not contain usable credentials, keeping raw development transcripts in source control creates a durable data-exposure surface for internal prompts, generated code attempts, command history, and any sensitive context that may be captured in future runs.
findings.fix: Remove the .conversations transcript from the PR, or replace it with a purpose-built, sanitized design note that contains only source-reviewable requirements and no model thinking, tool payloads, local execution details, credentials, or raw logs.
